description: Phase 10: Event Schema Registry, Notification Fanout, Idempotency Keys, and WS Gateway Integration. Add a central event schema registry with Zod-validated event definitions covering all cross-app events. Add notification fanout service that routes events to push/email/in-app based on user preferences. Add idempotency key middleware for write operations. Wire the ws-gateway service to use the realtime package auth, presence, and delivery systems.
steps: Step 1: Create packages/realtime/src/event-schema-registry.ts - Define a central EventSchemaRegistry class that maps event type strings to Zod schemas. Register schemas for all event types in EventMap (message:new, message:typing, message:read, presence:update, post:new, post:interaction, call:signal, call:incoming, stream:event, notification:new, ai:chunk, ai:device). Add validate() method. Add new event types: 'document:updated', 'file:shared', 'calendar:reminder', 'payment:received', 'search:invalidate'. Export from packages/realtime/src/index.ts.

Step 2: Create packages/notifications/src/services/notification-fanout.ts - Implement NotificationFanout class that accepts an event (from any app), determines recipients (via a resolver function), checks user preferences via PreferenceService, and routes to appropriate channels (in-app via UniversalNotificationCenter, push via PushService). Add mention detection logic. Export from packages/notifications/src/index.ts.

Step 3: Create packages/data-plane/src/idempotency.ts - Implement IdempotencyKeyStore class with set(key, result, ttlMs), get(key), and has(key) methods. Use in-memory Map with TTL cleanup (same pattern as other data-plane classes). Include IdempotencyMiddleware that wraps an async operation and returns cached result if key was seen within TTL. Export from packages/data-plane/src/index.ts.

Step 4: Update services/ws-gateway/src/main.ts to create a real WebSocket gateway using the @quant/realtime package. Import and use WebSocketServer, ConnectionAuth, PresenceManager, DeliveryManager, and ChannelManager. Set up JWT auth for upgrade requests. Set up presence tracking on connect/disconnect. Set up channel subscription routing. Keep the health server. Add ws-gateway dependency on @quant/realtime in its package.json.

Step 5: Create packages/queue/src/dead-letter.ts - Add a DeadLetterQueue class specifically for the queue package (BullMQ-oriented). Track failed jobs with metadata (original queue, error, attempts). Provide replay() method. Export from packages/queue/src/index.ts.

Step 6: Write tests - packages/realtime/src/__tests__/event-schema-registry.test.ts (validate known events, reject malformed), packages/notifications/src/__tests__/notification-fanout.test.ts (fanout routes to correct channels based on prefs, respects quiet hours), packages/data-plane/src/__tests__/idempotency.test.ts (stores result, returns cached, expires after TTL), packages/queue/src/__tests__/dead-letter.test.ts (enqueue failed jobs, replay, stats).
Step 7: Run pnpm typecheck, pnpm build, pnpm test, pnpm lint and fix any issues.
acceptance_criteria: EventSchemaRegistry validates all event types in the EventMap plus new cross-app events
NotificationFanout routes events to push/in-app/email channels respecting user preferences
IdempotencyKeyStore deduplicates write operations within TTL window
ws-gateway creates a real WebSocket server with JWT auth and presence tracking
DeadLetterQueue in queue package tracks failed jobs with replay capability
All new code has tests that exercise real logic paths
findings: ws-gateway needed project references in tsconfig.json to properly resolve @quant/realtime transitive imports with composite mode. Added references to ../../packages/realtime and ../../packages/common. All 4 quality gates pass: typecheck 72/72, build 51/51, test 75/75, lint 61/61.
